rulings.Case Title: BRIAN ROBERTS et al VS. ALFA LAVAL INC SUED INDIVIDUALLLY AND AS et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion To Compel Further Responses To Plaintiffs Special Interrogatories, Set One From Defendant Crane Co. And Request For Monetary Sanctions
rulings.Rulings: ON CALENDAR FOR TUESDAY, MARCH 23, 2010 IN DEPARTMENT 220 AT 9:30AM, LINE 1. Plaintiffs¿ motion to compel further responses to plaintiffs¿ special interrogatories, set one, and request for monetary sanctions is granted. Defendant Crane Co. failed to supply complete and responsive answers. ¿Where the question is specific and explicit, an answer that supplies only a portion of the information sought is wholly insufficient [¿] Also, a party may not deliberately misconstrue a question for the purpose of supplying an evasive answer, [¿] Answers must be complete and responsive.¿ Deyo v. Kilbourne (1978) 84 Cal.App.3d 771, 783-784. Defendant failed to identify and summarize the contents of the documents that formed the basis of its responses. Defendant also impermissibly re-defined interrogatories to limit the scope of its responses to ¿products identified by plaintiffs.¿ Defendant¿s arguments about the timing of discovery obligations lack merit. Defendant to provide full responses within 20 days. Since defendant failed to provide substantial justification for its actions plaintiff is entitled to sanctions of $1500. = (220/HEK)
